articles.title: ‚ÄúAlia Bhatt's work ethic is absolutely incredible": Alpha action director Craig Macrae says expect the unexpected from Alia Bhatt in Alpha
articles.description: As anticipation continues to build around Yash Raj Films Alpha, one of the film's key creative collaborators has opened up about the dedication and commitment that went into bringing its ambitious action sequences to life. Internationally acclaimed action director Craig Macrae, who has worked with some of the biggest global female action stars like Charlize Theron and Milla Jovovich, is full of praise for leading ladies Alia Bhatt and Sharvari, crediting their preparation, discipline and work ethic to pull off some really brutally hand to hand action sequences. Craig prepares us to expect the unexpected from Alia Bhatt. He says, "What sets Alia Bhatt apart from those other female stars is that I think her work ethic is absolutely incredible. She's really put in a lot of time and a lot of effort into preparing for Alpha. She was really dedicated to the movie and I think that's going to be the thing that really makes a huge difference in her on-screen presence." He further added that Alia's commitment extended beyond the film's demanding action requirements and into every aspect of her performance. "Not only just for the action preparation, she obviously did a lot of work for the preparation as the character as well. She's one of those people that you can really feel is willing to put in the amount of work that is required and then some, to go above and beyond what's needed for the film.‚ÄĚ Craig credit Alia and Sharvari‚Äôs brilliant team work to bring to life the action sequences that people will see in Alpha. "Alia and Sharvari were absolutely incredible for this movie. The preparation that they put into it, the work that they put into preparing for this movie physically and with their skills training and stunt training was absolutely incredible. It's very rare to find people who are willing to put in the amount of time and effort that Alia and Sharvari put into this movie,‚ÄĚ he says . Craig adds , ‚ÄúThat really made our lives a lot easier as action designers and stunt team because we had actors that were so well equipped and really well prepared for the action that we were designing in the film. It gave us a lot more ability to be able to design everything and anything that we wanted to in the movie knowing that the actors were going to be able to execute the action." Produced by Yash Raj Films and directed by Shiv Rawail, Alpha is set to release in theatres worldwide on July 3, 2026.
articles.source: Latest Movie Updates, Film News, Movie Reviews,pre

articles.title: Ebola Outbreak Faces Challenges As Traditional Healers Remain First Choice For Many
articles.description:
Efforts to contain Ebola outbreaks across parts of Africa have improved significantly in recent years. Better treatment facilities, stronger surveillance systems and expanded vaccination campaigns have sharpened the ability of health officials to respond quickly when new cases are reported. However, a significant obstacle still exists: a lot of people still turn to traditional healers for assistance before going to clinics or hospitals.

Medical professionals warn that this delay can slow the identification of Ebola cases, complicate contact tracing and make it far harder to prevent the virus from spreading through communities.

Enduring Trust In Traditional Healers

In many affected regions, traditional healers are not fringe figures - they are respected members of the community who provide herbal cures, spiritual assistance and wisdom steeped in local customs. For years, they have functioned as trusted sources of care, forging ties that formal healthcare institutions sometimes struggle to replicate.

ALSO READ |Doctors Reveal Surprising Diabetes Link Behind Frozen Shoulder

The preference for traditional healers is not simply a matter of distrust towards modern medicine. In rural areas, hospitals can be hours away, transport is often unaffordable, and health facilities regularly face staff and resource shortages. When illness first strikes, visiting a local healer is frequently the most practical option available. According to a report by Associated Press, traditional healers and religious leaders are often the first point of contact during health emergencies precisely because of the deep community trust they command.

‚ÄúWhen people do not trust the health system, they often go first to traditional healers, faith leaders, or people they already know. The danger is that many only reach the hospital when they are already very sick,‚ÄĚ AP quoted Onesphore Bangenza of the aid group Mercy Corps as saying.

Cultural Beliefs And Health-Seeking Behaviour

Cultural frameworks also shape how communities respond to disease. During past Ebola outbreaks, some families attributed unexplained illness to spiritual causes or ancestral forces rather than a virus. This is compounded by the fact that early Ebola symptoms - fever, fatigue, vomiting and diarrhoea - closely resemble those of more common illnesses like malaria or typhoid, making it difficult to identify without proper medical testing.

ALSO READ | Can Eating 50 Eggs At Once Be Deadly? Jaunpur Case Highlights Serious Risks

‚ÄúSome people still describe Ebola as something mysterious, spiritual, or brought by outsiders, rather than a disease that needs medical care,‚ÄĚ Bangenza further said.

The World Health Organization (WHO) notes that the disease can progress to severe complications, including internal and external bleeding.

Aid workers operating in outbreak zones have reportedly encountered communities where Ebola is sometimes understood through a spiritual or supernatural lens, leading some families to exhaust traditional remedies before seeking clinical care.

Why The Delay Matters

These delays carry serious consequences. According to the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Ebola spreads through direct contact with the bodily fluids of an infected person. Every day a patient goes undiagnosed, the people around them - family members, caregivers, neighbours - risk exposure. Faster identification is not just about treating the individual; it is the foundation of containing the outbreak entirely.

articles.title: When Dhurandhar Star Sara Arjun, 21, Called Sadhguru's Isha School Her "Second Home": "I Come Running Back"
articles.description: Sara Arjun at Isha Foundation during Shivratri (L) and Sadhguru (R).

articles.title: 'I came after giving 250 auditions': When 'Cocktail 2' star Shahid Kapoor shared his journey before stardom
articles.description: Bollywood star Shahid Kapoor revealed he never felt privileged despite his film family background, recounting years of auditions and financial struggles. He shared that early on, he couldn't even afford clothes in Lokhandwala market, highlighting his journey of perseverance. Kapoor is now gearing up for the release of 'Cocktail 2'.

articles.title: Drishyam 3 Telugu OTT release halted by Madras High Court after remake's director claims rights to Mohanlal-starrer
articles.description: Drishyam 3's Telugu OTT release is stalled due to a Madras High Court injunction, following a petition from filmmaker Sripriya's production company.

articles.title: Sara Arjun's father Raj Arjun pens heartfelt birthday note, calls her 'his world': 'Meri bachchi, aaj tumhari saalgirah hai'
articles.description: Actor Raj Arjun penned a heartfelt birthday tribute to his daughter, Sara Arjun, sharing an emotional note on social media. He described how her arrival brought immense joy and meaning to his life, transforming it with light and peace. Raj Arjun expressed that Sara is the answer to what life has given him, calling her his entire world. Read on to know more in detail.

articles.title: 'I Do Feel Bad For Him': Tanya Mittal Backs Pranit More Amid Ongoing 'Rs 370 Ki Biryani' Row
articles.description: Adding to the ongoing debate surrounding Pranit More, Bigg Boss 19 contestant Tanya Mittal has now weighed in on the controversy.
